verbGlossary.ACKNOWLEDGED BY: {source} formally confirms receipt and understanding of {target} or instruction, creating a compliance record.
verbGlossary.APPROVED BY: {source} grants formal acceptance of {target}, authorizing work to proceed based on its contents.
verbGlossary.ASSIGNED BY: {source} designates ownership of a task or action item to a responsible party for completion.
verbGlossary.AUTHORED BY: {source} originates and writes {target}, serving as the primary creator of its content.
verbGlossary.CAPTURED BY: {source} records visual documentation (photos or video) on site to document conditions, progress, or issues.
verbGlossary.CHAIRED BY: {source} leads and facilitates a meeting, setting the agenda and directing discussion.
verbGlossary.CLOSED OUT BY: {source} completes all remaining items on {target} and formally marks it as finished.
verbGlossary.COLLECTED BY: {source} gathers required documents from subcontractors or vendors to satisfy contractual or compliance obligations.
verbGlossary.COMPILED BY: {source} assembles multiple source documents into a consolidated deliverable package.
verbGlossary.COMPLETED BY: {source} fills out a required form or log entry with field data for the reporting period.
verbGlossary.CONFIGURED BY: {source} sets up document templates, workflows, or form structures for project use.
verbGlossary.COORDINATED BY: {source} aligns document content across multiple disciplines to resolve conflicts and ensure consistency.
verbGlossary.DISTRIBUTED BY: {source} routes {target} to the correct recipients per the project distribution matrix.
verbGlossary.DRAFTED BY: {target} prepares an initial version of {source} for review and approval by others.
verbGlossary.ENFORCED BY: {source} ensures compliance with a plan or document through field inspections and corrective actions.
verbGlossary.EXPEDITED BY: {source} accelerates {target} through its review cycle to prevent schedule delays.
verbGlossary.FILED BY: {source} archives {target} into the project record system for future reference and audit trails.
verbGlossary.INITIATED BY: {source} triggers the creation of {target} based on a field condition or project need.
verbGlossary.ISSUED BY: {source} formally releases {target} to the project team, establishing it as an official project record.
verbGlossary.LOGGED BY: {source} enters {target} into the project tracking system and assigns it a control number.
verbGlossary.MANAGED BY: {source} owns the end-to-end lifecycle of {target} type, from creation through closeout.
verbGlossary.MARKED UP BY: {source} annotates {target} with field conditions, corrections, or as-built information.
verbGlossary.MONITORED BY: {source} tracks a standard measure or KPI over time and flags deviations from acceptable thresholds.
verbGlossary.PRICED BY: {source} evaluates a directive or change and assigns cost and schedule impact values.
verbGlossary.REVIEWED BY: {source} examines {target} for accuracy, completeness, and compliance before it advances in workflow.
verbGlossary.SIGNED OFF BY: {source} provides final authority approval on a submittal or deliverable, clearing it for fabrication or procurement.
verbGlossary.TRACKED BY: {target} maintains status, revision history, and due-date compliance for {source} through its lifecycle.
verbGlossary.VERIFIED BY: {source} confirms that {target}'s contents match actual field conditions, contractual requirements, or insurance coverage.
